qa_pairs.question: Should applicants consult with technical staff before submitting their Resource Allocation Competition (RAC) application?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, it is strongly recommended that all applicants consult with technical staff responsible for the clusters they intend to use. This ensures the technical aspects of the proposals match well with the requested clusters, to the Principal Investigator’s benefit. Principal Investigators who have not previously used these clusters should discuss their proposal with technical staff before submission, and all consultations should happen in advance of completing the proposal to allow time for discussion or revisions.

qa_pairs.question: Is the Canadian Common CV (CCV) a mandatory requirement for applying to the Resource Allocation Competition (RAC)?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, Principal Investigators (PIs) and all Co-PIs of a Resource Allocation Competition (RAC) application are required to submit a Canadian Common CV (CCV). Integrating the CCV into the CCDB streamlines the process for applicants and helps the Scientific Review Committees evaluate the quality of each project’s research team.

qa_pairs.question: Are extensions granted for the final submission deadline of Resource Allocation Competition (RAC) applications?
qa_pairs.answer: No, the submission deadline for Resource Allocation Competition (RAC) applications is final. There will be no extensions provided, and late submissions will not be accepted.
